mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during structural labor shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price indices indicate strong demand outpacing supply, which forces corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production directly increases corporate output and profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns during stable rate periods, or the mechanism reverses direction when FEDFUNDS volatility exceeds 1.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment and higher aggregate wages, which boosts consumer demand and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of months with positive payroll growth, or the rolling Rank IC drops below 0.02 across multiple 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation surge above 0.4% triggers immediate market repricing of future rate hikes, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation months, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and real GDP signals synchronized expansion across residential and broader economic sectors, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but this mechanism only activates when unemployment is below 4.5%, indicating tight labor markets support wage-driven demand.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns when UNRATE is below 4.5%, or the signal reverses to negative during periods of acute supply chain bottlenecks.
